methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Picogrid, a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Zane Mountcastle, has raised $12.3M to date. Valuation assessment relies on two primary methods triangulated against a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the Defense & Security sector, including Tenna Systems, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Stage-Progression methodology applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, using total raised as a valuation proxy. This yields $31M. Peer-Median analysis examines four same-sector peers across Seed and Series A stages, with valuations spanning $60M to $500M and a median of $225M. This method implies $225M, though peer dispersion is material—the comp set includes both Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed stage) and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A).

Triangulation across both methods produces a range of $26M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $128M. The wide dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity in the peer set; Seed-stage comparable Tenna Systems trades significantly below Series A peers. Key uncertainties include Picogrid's technology maturity relative to peer cohort, commercial traction, and defensibility within a crowded defense-tech landscape. The Stage-Progression method assumes standard step-up dynamics; deviations would shift valuation materially.
